narrative: Trump's weekend threat of 25% tariffs on European autos and stalled US-Iran peace talks dominate the tape, setting up a defensive posture for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.1723; the tariff escalation and a +175bp carry disadvantage likely to cap upside despite recent DXY weakness. USD/JPY closed Friday at 157.03; safe-haven USD demand and a +300bp carry spread would support the pair if geopolitical friction persists. JPY/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Intervention: JPY 1W avg: +1.081% (n=7), USD 1W avg: -0.879% (n=7), G8 spread: 1.960%. Focus shifts to Tuesday's RBA meeting for the next high-impact catalyst.
